assertions.text: Both vehicles (V1 and V2) were traveling northbound on a four-lane, physically divided, bidirectional roadway with two lanes northbound and two lanes southbound.
Both vehicles were negotiating a right-hand curve in the roadway with a slight incline.
V1 was traveling in lane one (left-most northbound lane) and V2 was traveling in lane two (to the right of V1) prior to interaction.
V1 began to move (merge) into lane two while V2 was attempting to pass V1.
V2 departed its lane to the right prior to impact.
V2's frontal plane impacted V1's rear plane (V2 hit the rear of V1 from behind/front of V2 to rear of V1).
There was a single impact between V2 and V1 described (one collision where V2's front struck V1's rear).
After the impact, V2 came to final rest off the right side of the roadway.
There was a small fire in the engine compartment of V2 following the collision.
V1 continued northbound after the collision and was not located (V1 departed the scene/was not found on scene).
No specific travel speeds for V1 or V2 were reported in the narrative.
The narrative does not state that either vehicle braked prior to or during the collision.

Temporal ordering: V1 initiated movement into lane two, V2 attempted to pass, V2 departed its lane to the right, V2's front struck V1's rear, then V2 came to rest off the roadway and a small engine fire occurred; V1 continued northbound and was not located.
Severity: described effects were that V2 came to rest off the road and had a small engine compartment fire; severity otherwise not quantified.

verdicts.evidence: Road geometry and both-vehicle headings are not recorded in the vehicle telemetry; only V2 speed ["first_value": 121.0, "last_value": 111.0](T2) and EDR vehicle count ["vehicles_in_crash": 2](T1) are available, so the claim that both were northbound on a four-lane, physically divided roadway with two lanes each is unverifiable from V2 sensors alone.

Road curvature and grade are not captured by the vehicle sensors; steering shows a peak raw value of ["peak_raw_value": 30.0 deg at t=0.0](T2) but that does not prove a right-hand curve with a slight incline; the telemetry cannot confirm roadway curvature or incline.

Lane positions of V1 and V2 are not recorded by V2 telemetry. V2 speed samples show ["first_value": 121.0 km/h at t=-5.0 and last_value": 111.0 km/h at t=0.0](T2) but lane assignment (lane one vs lane two) is not available.

The sequence claiming V1 began to merge into V2's lane while V2 was passing requires observation of V1; V2 telemetry shows a brake application at ["t": -0.5s](T3) and steering peaks ["peak_raw_value": 30.0 deg at t=0.0](T2) but cannot establish V1's movement, so the merge intent is unverifiable.

V2 recorded a lateral delta-V peak during the crash pulse of ["value_at_max": -11.0 km/h at t=0.09 s](T2) indicating a rightward lateral velocity change during impact consistent with departing its lane to the right.

The EDR summary labels the event as ["event_desc": "Frontal / Rear Crash (Record 1)"](T1) and the crash-pulse longitudinal cumulative delta-V reached ["value_at_max": -81.0 km/h at t=0.16 s](T2) (negative indicating deceleration), supporting a forward-facing (front of V2) impact into another vehicle's rear.

The crash-pulse shows a single dominant longitudinal delta-V peak with magnitude ["value_at_max": -81.0 km/h](T2) and detect_events reported one delta-V impact detection for long axis ["magnitude_kmh": 81.0 at t=0.16](T3), consistent with a single impact recorded on V2.

Post-impact motion off-road is not directly logged, but V2 steering shows a peak at ["peak_raw_value": 30.0 deg at t=0.0](T2) and speed fell from ["first_value": 121.0 km/h at t=-5.0 to last_value": 111.0 km/h at t=0.0](T2) pre-impact; however the strongest sensor evidence is the lateral delta-V of ["value_at_max": -11.0 km/h at t=0.09 s](T2) indicating being pushed laterally to the right and coming to rest off the roadway is plausible but the final rest location off-road is unverifiable by sensors alone, so this assertion is partially supported for rightward departure during impact but final rest off-road is unverifiable.
Fire presence in engine compartment is not recorded in vehicle telemetry channels provided; no thermal or fire sensors are present, so the small engine fire claim is unverifiable.

Whether V1 continued northbound and was not located cannot be determined from V2 telemetry; EDR notes two vehicles in crash ["vehicles_in_crash": 2](T1) but V1's post-impact location is not recorded by V2 sensors.

The narrative indeed gives no specific travel speeds; telemetry shows V2 speed samples with ["first_value": 121.0 km/h at t=-5.0 and last_value": 111.0 km/h at t=0.0](T2), supporting that the narrative did not report explicit speeds for either vehicle.

The narrative states no braking was reported, but telemetry records a brake application transition at ["t": -0.5 s](T3) with brake fraction_on ["fraction_on": 0.18 and transition at t=-0.5 from 0 to 1](T2), contradicting the assertion that neither vehicle braked (at least V2 did).

The full temporal chain involves V1's actions which are not observable in V2 telemetry; V2 data shows a brake application at ["t": -0.5 s](T3), steering peak ["peak_raw_value": 30.0 deg at t=0.0](T2), and crash delta-V timings ["delta_v_lat" peak at 0.09 s and "delta_v_long" peak at 0.16 s](T2), but whether V1 initiated the sequence is unverifiable.

Telemetry shows a large longitudinal crash pulse of ["value_at_max": -81.0 km/h at t=0.16 s](T2) and an accompanying lateral delta-V of ["value_at_max": -11.0 km/h at t=0.09 s](T2); the narrative's qualitative description of V2 coming to rest off-road and a small engine fire is not fully quantifiable by sensors, but the recorded ["max_delta_v_long_kmh": -81.0](T1) supports a high-severity impact while the fire claim is unverifiable.

result.rationale: V2 telemetry definitively shows a large rear/frontal impact for V2 (longitudinal delta-V ["-81.0 km/h" at t=0.16 s](T2)), a lateral departure to the right (lateral delta-V ["-11.0 km/h" at t=0.09 s](T2)), and a brake application before impact ["t": -0.5 s](T3). Road geometry, V1 movements, fire presence, and final rest location off-road are not fully recorded by V2 sensors and are therefore unverifiable.

citations.claim: "first_value": 121.0 km/h at t=-5.0 and last_value": 111.0 km/h at t=0.0
"first_value": 121.0 km/h at t=-5.0 to last_value": 111.0 km/h at t=0.0
"first_value": 121.0 km/h at t=-5.0 and last_value": 111.0 km/h at t=0.0
"fraction_on": 0.18 and transition at t=-0.5 from 0 to 1
"delta_v_lat" peak at 0.09 s and "delta_v_long" peak at 0.16 s

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

accel_pedal.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

detections.criterion: crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum
crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
